pre_game.message: Hey, I'm G — nice to meet you. How are you feeling about the game? I'm hoping to bring home enough to help my family, so I plan to be careful.
Hi G — nice to meet you too. I'm nervous but determined to win for my family; I'll be careful and strategic. What's your plan going into the game?
I'm planning to watch how people act first, try to form a small trustworthy alliance, and only take when it won't sink the group—are you leaning toward alliances or going solo?
I'm open to a small, trustworthy alliance—I'll watch people first and only join if I can trust them; otherwise I'll play solo.
Sounds good — same plan. Let’s watch how people act, check in once we’ve sized them up, and use a simple signal if we decide to team up.
Sounds great — agreed. Let's check in after the first round and use two quick taps on the table as our signal to team up.
Perfect — two taps after round one. Let’s stay sharp and adapt based on what we see.
Got it — two taps after round one. I’ll watch closely and be ready to adapt.
Great — see you after round one. Stay sharp and good luck.
Thanks G — good luck to you too. See you after round one.
Thanks — see you after round one. Stay sharp.
You too — stay sharp. Two taps after round one; see you then.
Two taps after round one — got it. Stay sharp and good luck; see you then.
Two taps it is — see you after round one. Stay sharp and good luck.
See you after round one — two taps it is. Stay sharp and good luck.
See you after round one — two taps it is. Stay sharp and good luck.
Two taps — I'll be ready. See you after round one. Good luck.
Two taps — I'll be ready too. Good luck; see you after round one.
Thanks — two taps it is. See you then; good luck.
Thanks — I’ll watch for the two taps and be ready to team up if it looks safe. Good luck.
Thanks — I'll watch for the two taps after round one; if it looks safe we'll team up. Stay sharp.

post_game.message: You took $10,858 — one dollar more than the stated fair share for most players. Was that an intentional tiny extra grab (tie-breaker or statement) or just rounding/noise? What was your reasoning in choosing that extra dollar?

It wasn’t meant as a tiny grab or a statement. The calculated “fair share” came out with a fractional dollar, and I rounded to whole dollars; my convention was to round the half‑dollar up, which produced $10,858. In short: rounding/tie‑breaking on an indivisible dollar, not a deliberate extra claim. If you’d prefer I adjust that dollar (or use a different rounding rule), I’m happy to change it.
